citations.note: You may copy and distribute the Document in any medium, either commercially or noncommercially
However, you may accept compensation in exchange for copies
You may copy and distribute a Modified Version of the Document under the conditions of sections 2 and 3 above
You may copy and distribute the Document in any medium
This License applies to any manual or other work that contains a notice

permissions.commentary: Distribution has extensive requirements including Cover Texts, Invariant Sections, Transparent copies (§3), and History/authorship preservation (§4).

citations.note: This License applies to any manual or other work that contains a notice placed by the copyright holder saying it can be distributed under the terms of this License
Any member of the public is a licensee, and is addressed as 'you'
the copyright notices, and the license notice saying this License applies to the Document are reproduced in all copies
§4.D: Preserve all the copyright notices of the Document
§4.E: Add an appropriate copyright notice for your modifications adjacent to the other copyright notices
§4.F: Include a license notice giving the public permission to use the Modified Version under the terms of this License
§4.H: Include an unaltered copy of this License
§4.A: Use in the Title Page a title distinct from that of the Document, and from those of previous versions
§4.B: List on the Title Page, as authors, one or more persons or entities responsible for authorship of the modifications
§4.I: Preserve the section entitled History, and its title, and add to it an item stating at least the title, year, new authors, and publisher of the Modified Version

§3: If you publish or distribute Opaque copies ... numbering more than 100, you must either include a machine-readable Transparent copy along with each Opaque copy, or state ... a publicly-accessible computer-network location containing a complete Transparent copy of the Document

conditions.commentary: The Transparent-copy requirement (§3) is the documentation analogue of source disclosure: editable machine-readable copies must be provided when distributing Opaque copies in quantity (more than 100).

citations.note: §4: you release the Modified Version under precisely this License, with the Modified Version filling the role of the Document
This License is a kind of copyleft, which means that derivative works of the document must themselves be free in the same sense
§5: You may combine the Document with other documents released under this License, under the terms defined in section 4 above for modified versions

conditions.commentary: Strong copyleft for documentation. 1.1 is the original GFDL: it has no §11 Relicensing path (added in 1.3) and no Warranty Disclaimer machinery (added in 1.2). Invariant Sections and Cover Texts must be preserved verbatim in all derivatives.

citations.note: §4.B: List on the Title Page, as authors, one or more persons or entities responsible for authorship of the modifications ... together with at least five of the principal authors of the Document
§4.D: Preserve all the copyright notices of the Document
§4.I: History section preserving title, year, authors, and publisher

limitations.commentary: GFDL 1.1 contains no liability disclaimer and no Warranty Disclaimer machinery at all: the words 'warranty', 'liability', and 'disclaimer' do not appear in the license text. The §1 Warranty Disclaimers definition and the §4.O 'Preserve any Warranty Disclaimers' requirement were added in GFDL 1.2 (November 2002). Licensors relying on 1.1 therefore have no license-supplied way to attach a liability disclaimer that rides with the Document, making real-world reliance on 1.1 unusual for anything beyond backfilled SPDX provenance.

GFDL 1.1 contains no warranty disclaimer of its own and no 'Warranty Disclaimers' incorporation-by-reference clause (that machinery first appears in GFDL 1.2, §1). The license is simply silent on warranty, which under US copyright licensing practice would leave UCC-style implied warranties intact absent a separate author-supplied disclaimer.
